verses.book: How the Ruler, While Living Well, Ought to Teach and Admonish Those that are Put Under Him.
verses.text: Differently to be admonished are these that follow:—

verses.book: How the Ruler, While Living Well, Ought to Teach and Admonish Those that are Put Under Him.
verses.text: The poor and the rich.

verses.book: How the Ruler, While Living Well, Ought to Teach and Admonish Those that are Put Under Him.
verses.text: The joyful and the sad.

verses.book: How the Ruler, While Living Well, Ought to Teach and Admonish Those that are Put Under Him.
verses.text: Prelates and subordinates.

verses.book: How the Ruler, While Living Well, Ought to Teach and Admonish Those that are Put Under Him.
verses.text: Servants and masters.

verses.book: How the Ruler, While Living Well, Ought to Teach and Admonish Those that are Put Under Him.
verses.text: The wise of this world and the dull.

verses.book: How the Ruler, While Living Well, Ought to Teach and Admonish Those that are Put Under Him.
verses.text: The impudent and the bashful.

verses.book: How the Ruler, While Living Well, Ought to Teach and Admonish Those that are Put Under Him.
verses.text: The forward and the fainthearted.

verses.book: How the Ruler, While Living Well, Ought to Teach and Admonish Those that are Put Under Him.
verses.text: The impatient and the patient.

verses.book: How the Ruler, While Living Well, Ought to Teach and Admonish Those that are Put Under Him.
verses.text: The kindly disposed and the envious.

verses.book: How the Ruler, While Living Well, Ought to Teach and Admonish Those that are Put Under Him.
verses.text: The simple and the insincere.

verses.book: How the Ruler, While Living Well, Ought to Teach and Admonish Those that are Put Under Him.
verses.text: The whole and the sick.

verses.book: How the Ruler, While Living Well, Ought to Teach and Admonish Those that are Put Under Him.
verses.text: Those who fear scourges, and therefore live innocently; and those who have grown so hard in iniquity as not to be corrected even by scourges.

verses.book: How the Ruler, While Living Well, Ought to Teach and Admonish Those that are Put Under Him.
verses.text: The too silent, and those who spend time in much speaking.

verses.book: How the Ruler, While Living Well, Ought to Teach and Admonish Those that are Put Under Him.
verses.text: The slothful and the hasty.

verses.book: How the Ruler, While Living Well, Ought to Teach and Admonish Those that are Put Under Him.
verses.text: The meek and the passionate.

verses.book: How the Ruler, While Living Well, Ought to Teach and Admonish Those that are Put Under Him.
verses.text: The humble and the haughty.

verses.book: How the Ruler, While Living Well, Ought to Teach and Admonish Those that are Put Under Him.
verses.text: The obstinate and the fickle.

verses.book: How the Ruler, While Living Well, Ought to Teach and Admonish Those that are Put Under Him.
verses.text: The gluttonous and the abstinent.

verses.book: How the Ruler, While Living Well, Ought to Teach and Admonish Those that are Put Under Him.
verses.text: Those who mercifully give of their own, and those who would fain seize what belongs to others.

verses.book: How the Ruler, While Living Well, Ought to Teach and Admonish Those that are Put Under Him.
verses.text: Those who neither seize the things of others nor are bountiful with their own; and those who both give away the things they have, and yet cease not to seize the things of others.

verses.book: How the Ruler, While Living Well, Ought to Teach and Admonish Those that are Put Under Him.
verses.text: Those that are at variance, and those that are at peace.

verses.book: How the Ruler, While Living Well, Ought to Teach and Admonish Those that are Put Under Him.
verses.text: Lovers of strifes and peacemakers.

verses.book: How the Ruler, While Living Well, Ought to Teach and Admonish Those that are Put Under Him.
verses.text: Those that understand not aright the words of sacred law; and those who understand them indeed aright, but speak them without humility.

verses.book: How the Ruler, While Living Well, Ought to Teach and Admonish Those that are Put Under Him.
verses.text: Those who, though able to preach worthily, are afraid through excessive humility; and those whom imperfection or age debars from preaching, and yet rashness impels to it.

verses.book: How the Ruler, While Living Well, Ought to Teach and Admonish Those that are Put Under Him.
verses.text: Those who prosper in what they desire in temporal matters; and those who covet indeed the things that are of the world, and yet are wearied with the toils of adversity.

verses.book: How the Ruler, While Living Well, Ought to Teach and Admonish Those that are Put Under Him.
verses.text: Those who are bound by wedlock, and those who are free from the ties of wedlock.

verses.book: How the Ruler, While Living Well, Ought to Teach and Admonish Those that are Put Under Him.
verses.text: Those who have had experience of carnal intercourse, and those who are ignorant of it.

verses.book: How the Ruler, While Living Well, Ought to Teach and Admonish Those that are Put Under Him.
verses.text: Those who deplore sins of deed, and those who deplore sins of thought.

verses.book: How the Ruler, While Living Well, Ought to Teach and Admonish Those that are Put Under Him.
verses.text: Those who bewail misdeeds, yet forsake them not; and those who forsake them, yet bewail them not.

verses.book: How the Ruler, While Living Well, Ought to Teach and Admonish Those that are Put Under Him.
verses.text: Those who even praise the unlawful things they do; and those who censure what is wrong, yet avoid it not.

verses.book: How the Ruler, While Living Well, Ought to Teach and Admonish Those that are Put Under Him.
verses.text: Those who are overcome by sudden passion, and those who are bound in guilt of set purpose.

verses.book: How the Ruler, While Living Well, Ought to Teach and Admonish Those that are Put Under Him.
verses.text: Those who, though their unlawful deeds are trivial, yet do them frequently; and those who keep themselves from small sins, but are occasionally whelmed in graver ones.

verses.book: How the Ruler, While Living Well, Ought to Teach and Admonish Those that are Put Under Him.
verses.text: Those who do not even begin what is good, and those who fail entirely to complete the good begun.

verses.book: How the Ruler, While Living Well, Ought to Teach and Admonish Those that are Put Under Him.
verses.text: Those who do evil secretly and good publicly; and those who conceal the good they do, and yet in some things done publicly allow evil to be thought of them.

verses.book: How the Ruler, While Living Well, Ought to Teach and Admonish Those that are Put Under Him.
verses.text: But of what profit is it for us to run through all these things collected together in a list, unless we also set forth, with all possible brevity, the modes of admonition for each?

verses.book: How the Ruler, While Living Well, Ought to Teach and Admonish Those that are Put Under Him.
verses.text: (Admonition 1.)

verses.book: How the Ruler, While Living Well, Ought to Teach and Admonish Those that are Put Under Him.
verses.text: Differently, then, to be admonished are men and women; because on the former heavier injunctions, on the latter lighter are to be laid, that those may be exercised by great things, but these winningly converted by light ones.

verses.book: How the Ruler, While Living Well, Ought to Teach and Admonish Those that are Put Under Him.
verses.text: (Admonition 2.)

verses.book: How the Ruler, While Living Well, Ought to Teach and Admonish Those that are Put Under Him.
verses.text: Differently to be admonished are young men and old; because for the most part severity of admonition directs the former to improvement, while kind remonstrance disposes the latter to better deeds.

verses.book: How the Ruler, While Living Well, Ought to Teach and Admonish Those that are Put Under Him.
verses.text: For it is written, Rebuke not an elder, but entreat him as a father (1 Tim. v.
